title: Remember More Effectively: - Think Deeply: Relate New Info to Something Personal or Meaningful
subtitle: Deepen Memory with Levels of Processing
description:
To remember more effectively:
- Think deeply: Relate new info to something personal or meaningful.
- Visualize: Picture concepts or create mental stories about them.
- Engage actively: Rephrase or discuss what you’re learning.

Example: Instead of memorizing “photosynthesis,” think about how it powers the food you eat and describe it to a friend.
keywords: Deepen Memory with Levels of Processing

howTo.title: Connect new information to personal experiences
howTo.summary: Spend 2 minutes linking a new fact to a personal memory, goal, or project. This enhances encoding by creating meaningful associations.

howTo.title: Visualize the concept vividly
howTo.summary: Take 2 minutes to create a sensory-rich mental image or short scene that embodies the concept. Use movement and at least two senses for better recall.

howTo.title: Rephrase and explain the idea
howTo.summary: Spend 4 minutes articulating the concept in your own words. Explain it as if teaching someone else, which reinforces understanding and retention.

howTo.title: Schedule retrieval reminders
howTo.summary: Set reminders for 6 hours and 24 hours after encoding to review the material. This spaced retrieval significantly boosts memory retention.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your deep encodings and set reminders for future retrievals. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/deepen-memory-levels-of-processing/.
